# Startup & Admin
## Reading in libraries, setting up, reading in data (run this chunk FIRST)
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(rcompanion)
library(ggplot2) # used to make plots
library(cowplot) # used to make aesthetically beautiful plots, on Emerson's recommendation
library(gridExtra) # used to make multipanel figures from individual figures
library(ggbiplot) # to make PCAs using ggplot2
library(ARTool) # used to conduct non-parametric ANOVA
library(emmeans) # to do pairwise comparisons
library(Hmisc) # to make a spearman correlation matrix
library(FSA) # to do Dunn test
library(rstatix) # to do Dunn test
library(multcompView) # to do Dunn test
library(scales) # to set increments on y axis
library(recolorize) # to read in the legend
library(grid) # for making multipanel figure
library(gtable) # for making multipanel figure
library(tidyverse) # for making spearman correlation matrices


# === FILE PATHS (update these if running on another machine) ===
dropbox     <- "/Users/emmadaily/BOSTON UNIVERSITY Dropbox/Emma Daily/Lab Files/Projects/UNE/Particulate Matter Project/Daily PM Analysis/RCode"
project     <- file.path(dropbox, "Lab Files/Projects/UNE/Particulate Matter Project/Daily PM Analysis/RCode/PMComp")
data_dir    <- file.path(project, "csvs")
figures_dir <- file.path(project, "Figures")
stats_dir <- file.path(project, "Statistics Results")

# Setting working directory to folder "csvs" to read in all the data

  setwd(data_dir)
  # Reading in axes limits from .csv
  axes_limits <- read.csv("axes_limits.csv")
  axes_limits_medians <- read.csv("axes_limits_medians.csv")

# Reading in Data
  
  # Reading In Leaf PM Composition Data

  FoliarPMComp <- read.csv("LeafPMComp.csv")
  # complete.cases(FoliarPMComp[,12]) # identifying rows with NAs in the "C" column
  FoliarPMComp <- FoliarPMComp[c(1:200,202:206,209:456),] # omitting those rows
  FoliarPMComp$K <- as.numeric(FoliarPMComp$K) # coercing K to numeric (NAs throw off the read-in)
  FoliarPMComp$K <- ifelse(is.na(FoliarPMComp$K), 0, FoliarPMComp$K) # replacing NAs with 0s in K
  FoliarPMComp$dfe <- as.factor(FoliarPMComp$distance_from_edge.m.)

  # Reading In Ambient PM Composition Data
    # this is the dataframe created in the chunk below, "Reading in Passive Sampler PM Composition Data"
  AmbientPMComp <- read.csv("AmbientPM.csv")

  # Reading in info about the UNE Sites
  UNESites <- read.csv("UNE_Sites.csv")

  # Combining site info with these two dataframes
  FoliarPMCompxUNESites <- left_join(FoliarPMComp,UNESites,by="UNE_site")
  AmbientPMCompxUNESites <- left_join(AmbientPMComp,UNESites,by="UNE_site")
  
  # converting everything to factors that needs to be converted
  FoliarPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_isa <- as.factor(FoliarPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_isa)
  FoliarPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_dist <- as.factor(FoliarPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_dist)
  FoliarPMCompxUNESites$edge_type <- as.factor(FoliarPMCompxUNESites$edge_type)
  
  AmbientPMCompxUNESites$dfe <- as.factor(AmbientPMCompxUNESites$dfe)
  AmbientPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_isa <- as.factor(AmbientPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_isa)
  AmbientPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_dist <- as.factor(AmbientPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_dist)
  AmbientPMCompxUNESites$edge_type <- as.factor(AmbientPMCompxUNESites$edge_type)
  
# Steve's function to calculate mean and standard error for variable of interest, which will be important for all these stats and figures
# data_summary <- function(data, varname, groupnames){
  #require(dplyr)
#   summary_func <- function(x, col){
#     c(mean = mean(x[[col]], na.rm=TRUE),
#       se = sd(x[[col]], na.rm=TRUE) / sqrt(length(x[[col]])))
#   }
#   data_sum <- ddply(data, groupnames, .fun=summary_func,
#                   varname)
#   data_sum <- rename(data_sum, c("mean" = varname))
#   return(data_sum)
# }

# updated version of this function
data_summary <- function(data, varname, groupnames){
  varname <- as.character(varname)

  data %>%
    summarise(
      n = sum(!is.na(.data[[varname]])),
      mean_val = mean(.data[[varname]], na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_val = sd(.data[[varname]], na.rm = TRUE),
      .by = all_of(groupnames)
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      se = sd_val / sqrt(n)
    ) %>%
    select(-sd_val, -n) %>%
    rename(!!varname := mean_val)
}

# Emma's function to calculate std error
  stderr <- function(x, na.rm = FALSE){
    se = sd(x) / sqrt(length(x))
    print(se)
}

# Emma's function to calculate the 95% confidence interval
ci95_median_boot <- function(x, conf = 0.95, R = 5000, na.rm = TRUE) {
  if (na.rm) x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  
  boot_medians <- replicate(R, {
    median(sample(x, replace = TRUE))
  })
  
  alpha <- 1 - conf
  lower <- quantile(boot_medians, alpha/2)
  upper <- quantile(boot_medians, 1 - alpha/2)
  
  return(c(
    median = median(x),
    lower = lower,
    upper = upper
  ))
}
```

## Grouping Elements (run this chunk SECOND)
```{r}
# IMPORTANT NOTE: if you need to re-run any part of this chunk, a) re-run the first chunk before doing so to ensure consistent column numbers, and b) re-run this entire chunk and not just one line. 

# Foliar

    # creating a summary column for cations
    
    FoliarPMCompxUNESites$"cations" <- rowSums(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[,c(20,16,17,23)]) # this includes Ca, Na, Mg, and K (Conrad-Rooney 2023)
    
    # creating a summary column for metals
    
    FoliarPMCompxUNESites$"metals" <- rowSums(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[,c(25:30)]) # this includes Cr, Mn, Fe, Ni, Zn, Cu 
    
    # creating a summary column for traffic-related metals
      # list of traffic-associated metals: Cu, Mn, Fe, Zn, Ba, Sn, Ni, Mo and Sb (list from Pant and Harrison 2013 review)
      # for this suite: Cu, Mn, Fe, Zn, Ni, Mo
    
    FoliarPMCompxUNESites$"traffic" <- rowSums(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[,c(29,26,27,30,28,31)])
    
    # calculating C:N ratio
    
    FoliarPMCompxUNESites$"CN" <- FoliarPMCompxUNESites$C/FoliarPMCompxUNESites$N
    FoliarPMCompxUNESites$"CN"[is.infinite(FoliarPMCompxUNESites$"CN")] <- NA # replacing "divided by zero" Inf values with NAs
    FoliarPMCompxUNESites$"CN"[FoliarPMCompxUNESites$"CN" == 0] <- NA # replacing zero values (for samples that had no N) with NAs
    
    # reordering the dataframe so these new columns come right after the elements (for the loops later)
    
    FoliarPMCompxUNESites <- FoliarPMCompxUNESites[,c(1:9,11,10,12:33,42:45,34:41)]
    

# Ambient

    # creating a summary column for metals
    
    AmbientPMCompxUNESites$"metals" <- rowSums(AmbientPMCompxUNESites[,c(19:24)]) # this includes Cr, Mn, Fe, Ni, Zn, Cu 
    
    # creating a summary column for cations
    
    AmbientPMCompxUNESites$"cations" <- rowSums(AmbientPMCompxUNESites[,c(17,9,10,16)]) # this includes Ca, Na, Mg, and K (Conrad-Rooney2023)
    
    # creating a summary column for traffic-related metals
      # list of traffic-associated metals: Cu, Mn, Fe, Zn, Ni, Mo (subset of list from Pant and Harrison 2013 review)
    
    AmbientPMCompxUNESites$"traffic" <- rowSums(AmbientPMCompxUNESites[,c(23,20,21,24,22,27)])
    
    # calculating C:N ratio
    
    AmbientPMCompxUNESites$"CN" <- AmbientPMCompxUNESites$C/AmbientPMCompxUNESites$N
    AmbientPMCompxUNESites$"CN"[is.infinite(AmbientPMCompxUNESites$"CN")] <- NA # replacing "divided by zero" Inf values with NAs
    AmbientPMCompxUNESites$"CN"[AmbientPMCompxUNESites$"CN" == 0] <- NA # replacing zero values (for samples that had no N) with NAs
    
    # creating a summary column for traffic-related metals
      # list of traffic-associated metals (including those not included in the foliar set): Cu, Mn, Fe, Zn, Ba, Sn, Ni, Mo and Sb (subset of list from Pant and Harrison 2013 review)
    
    AmbientPMCompxUNESites$"traffic_extra" <- rowSums(AmbientPMCompxUNESites[,c(23,20,21,24,30,28,22,27,29)])
    
    # rearranging the dataset so that these columns come right after the other elements
    
    AmbientPMCompxUNESites <- AmbientPMCompxUNESites[,c(1:44,54:58,45:53)]
    

```

## Combining Ambient and Foliar Data (run this chunk THIRD)
```{r}
# make new versions of each dataframe which include a column noting particle source (ambient or foliar)

foliar_to_combine <- FoliarPMCompxUNESites
foliar_to_combine$particle_source <- "Foliar"

ambient_to_combine <- AmbientPMCompxUNESites
ambient_to_combine$particle_source <- "Ambient"

# identifying which columns the ambient and foliar element data have in common (should be about half the elements, because ambient PM had a larger suite of elements analyzed, as well as the UNE site info and our new particle_source column). check to make sure nothing is missing, then make these columns into a vector.

incommon <- intersect(colnames(foliar_to_combine), colnames(ambient_to_combine))

AllCompxUNESites <- rbind(foliar_to_combine[,incommon], ambient_to_combine[,incommon])

AllCompxUNESites$particle_source <- as.factor(AllCompxUNESites$particle_source)

setwd(data_dir)
write.csv(AllCompxUNESites,"AllCompxUNESites.csv", row.names=FALSE)

# defining list of element names
        foliar_element_names <- colnames(FoliarPMCompxUNESites)[11:37]
        ambient_element_names <- colnames(AmbientPMCompxUNESites)[5:48]
        all_element_names <- colnames(FoliarPMCompxUNESites)[12:37]

```

## Reading in Ambient PM Composition Data
```{r, eval=FALSE}
# NOTE: Don't run this chunk again unless you actually need to! It was a one-time function to produce the file AmbientPM.csv (read in in the chunk above).

# Before we think about using the PM composition data from the passive samplers, we have to extract it from the 44 separate Excel files it's currently in, format them identically, and make them into a single (long) data frame.

install.packages("readxl")
library("readxl") # to handle inputting the Excel files

setwd(data_dir)

# Creating a loop to extract data from all 44 different Excel files

    # This is the generic filepath for the .xlsx files:
    
    "/Users/emmadaily/BOSTON UNIVERSITY Dropbox/Emma Daily/Lab Files/Projects/UNE/Particulate Matter Project/Rindy PM Study/SEM Passive Sampler Data and Photos/EDS Data/AA01_mid_eds/AA01_001/reports/AA01_001_data"
    
    # this creates a list of sample names that exist, to plug into the generic filepath
    
    file_names <- c("AA01_001","AA01_002","AA01_901","AA01_902","AA01_open1","AA01_open2",
                    "BH02_001","BH02_002","BH02_901","BH02_902","BH02_open1","BH02_open2",
                    "BM03_001","BM03_002","BM03_901","BM03_902","BM03_open1","BM03_open2",
                    "HF04_001","HF04_002","HF04_901","HF04_902","HF04_open1","HF04_open2",
                    "HF05_001","HF05_002","HF05_901","HF05_902",
                    "HF06_001","HF06_002","HF06_901","HF06_902",
                    "HW07_001","HW07_002","HW07_901","HW07_902","HW07_open1","HW07_open2",
                    "SW08_001","SW08_002","SW08_901","SW08_902","SW08_open1","SW08_open2"
                    )
    
    # making a file with all the filepaths in it
    
    file_paths <- paste0("/Users/emmadaily/BOSTON UNIVERSITY Dropbox/Emma Daily/Lab Files/Projects/UNE/Particulate Matter Project/Rindy PM Study/SEM Passive Sampler Data and Photos/EDS Data/",substr(file_names, 1, 4),"_mid_eds/",file_names,"/reports/",file_names,"_data.xlsx")
    
    # testing the path that will be used in the loop
    
      test <- read_excel("/Users/emmadaily/BOSTON UNIVERSITY Dropbox/Emma Daily/Lab Files/Projects/UNE/Particulate Matter Project/Rindy PM Study/SEM Passive Sampler Data and Photos/EDS Data/AA01_mid_eds/AA01_001/reports/AA01_001_data.xlsx")  
      temp_df <- read_excel(file_paths[28],col_names = FALSE) # reading in the excel file
      temp_df <- temp_df[9:38,] # selecting only the data and not the summary the company provided
      temp_df <- t(temp_df) # rotating the dataframe 
      temp_df <- temp_df %>% replace(is.na(.), 0) # replacing all NAs with zeroes
      colnames(temp_df) <- temp_df[1,] # making the column names from the first row
      temp_df <- temp_df[-1,] # removing the first row
      temp_df <- as.data.frame(temp_df) # ensuring it's a data frame in order to add columns
      temp_df$sampleID <- file_names[28] # adding a column called "sample" with the name of the sample
      temp_df$spectrum <- paste0("Spectrum ",seq_len(nrow(temp_df))) # adding a column with the spectrum number (e.g. Spectrum 1, Spectrum 2, etc)
      
    # making a loop to read in 44 different Excel files with these names, and store them as objects in R
    
      # making an empty list to store the data frames
      
       dfs <- list()
       
      # now for the loop
        for (i in seq_along(file_paths)) {
      
            temp_df <- read_excel(file_paths[i],col_names = FALSE) 
              temp_df <- temp_df[9:38,] 
              temp_df <- t(temp_df) 
              temp_df <- temp_df[rowSums(is.na(temp_df)) != ncol(temp_df), ] 
              temp_df <- temp_df %>% replace(is.na(.), 0)
              colnames(temp_df) <- temp_df[1,] 
              temp_df <- temp_df[-1,] 
              temp_df <- as.data.frame(temp_df) 
              temp_df$sample <- file_names[i] 
              temp_df$spectrum <- paste0("Spectrum ",seq_len(nrow(temp_df)))
              dfs[[i]] <- temp_df
        }
       
       # synthesizes all 44 dataframes into a single dataframe, by the name of the column
       # columns (which represent elements) are only present in the sample dataframes when at least one particle from that sample has the compound present. the bind_rows function fills the cells with NAs when the sample does not have that column present. 
       
       AmbientPM <- bind_rows(dfs)

      # now we can clean up the big dataframe 
       
        rownames(AmbientPM) <- 1:968 # renames the rows to simple numbers
        AmbientPM <- AmbientPM[, c(31,32,1:30,33:51)] # bringing the columns with sample and site ID to the front
        AmbientPM <- AmbientPM %>% mutate_at(c(3:51), as.numeric) # converting the rest of the columns to numeric
        AmbientPM <- AmbientPM[,c(1:31,39,41:51)] # removing some columns that randomly appeared but are empty (I confirmed this using the command max())
        AmbientPM <- AmbientPM[,c(1:30,32:43,31)] # "Total" column to the back
        AmbientPM <- AmbientPM %>% replace(is.na(.), 0) # replaces all NAs with zeroes (see above)
        
      # adding some helpful columns
        AmbientPM$Total_Check <- rowSums(AmbientPM[,3:42]) # a column at the end with the sum of all other columns

        # adding "dfe" as a variable based on sample name
        sitecode_mapping <- c(AA01="AA01",BH02="BH02",BM03="BM03",HF04="HF04",HF05="HF05",HF06="HF06",HW07="HW07",SW08="SW08")
        samplecode_mapping <- c("001"="0","002"="0","901"="90","902"="90","open1"="Open","open2"="Open")
        AmbientPM <- AmbientPM %>%
          separate(sample, into = c("sitecode", "samplecode"), sep = "_", remove = FALSE) %>%
          mutate(UNE_site = sitecode_mapping[sitecode],
                 dfe = samplecode_mapping[samplecode],) %>%
                  select(-sitecode, -samplecode)
        AmbientPM <- AmbientPM[,c(1,45:46,2:44)] # bringing that column to the front
        
        # adding sampler number to the the dataset
        
        AmbientPM$sampler <- substr(AmbientPM$sample,nchar(AmbientPM$sample),nchar(AmbientPM$sample))
      
        # write this dataframe into a csv and save it in the working directory
        write.csv(AmbientPM, file.path(data_dir,"AmbientPM.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```

# Individual Element Analysis
## Non-parametric Aligned Ranks Transformation ANOVA & Kruskal-Wallis Test
### Urban/Rural and Edge/Interior
#### Ambient & Foliar
##### ART ANOVA (includes open sites for ambient)
```{r}
# Foliar
    
    # create an empty matrix, with a row for each element and a column each for: p-value on edge vs. interior, p-value for urban vs rural (by dist), p-value for interaction term (by dist), p-value for urban vs rural (by %ISA), p-value for interaction term (by %ISA)
    
        foliar_PM_art_pvals <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=6)
        colnames(foliar_PM_art_pvals) <- c("Element","Edge_vs_Interior","Urb_v_Rural_Dist","Interaction_Dist","Urb_v_Rural_ISA","Interaction_ISA" )
        foliar_PM_art_pvals[,1] <- all_element_names
        
    # create an empty matrix, with a row for each element and a column for each pairwise comparison
        
        foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=11)
        foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Rural,0 - Rural,90",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,90",
            "Urban,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural0",
            "Rural90",
            "Urban0",
            "Urban90"
            )
        
        foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=11)
        foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Rural,0 - Rural,90",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,90",
            "Urban,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural0",
            "Rural90",
            "Urban0",
            "Urban90"
            )
        
    # now the matrices are ready to go, and we can fill it with these p-values using the loop below
    
    for (i in c(1:26)) {
      
    element <- all_element_names[i]
    no.nas <- AmbientPMCompxUNESites[complete.cases(AmbientPMCompxUNESites[, element]), ]
    

    
    # ART Anova
        
      # Edge vs. Interior
      formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~ dfe"))
      X_ART_EvI <- art(formula, data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),])
      X_aovART_EvI <- anova(X_ART_EvI)
      X_aovART_EvI_pval <- X_aovART_EvI["dfe","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for edge vs. interior
      foliar_PM_art_pvals[i,2] <- X_aovART_EvI_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
      
      # Urban vs. Rural
        # dist
        formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_dist"))
        X_ART_dist <- art(formula, data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),])
        X_aovART_dist <- anova(X_ART_dist)
        U_R_art_dist_pval <- X_aovART_dist["urb_cat_dist","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for urban vs. rural
        foliar_PM_art_pvals[i,3] <- U_R_art_dist_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
        
        # isa
        formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_isa"))
        X_ART_isa <- art(formula, data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),])
        X_aovART_isa <- anova(X_ART_isa)
        U_R_art_isa_pval <- X_aovART_isa["urb_cat_isa","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for urban vs. rural
        foliar_PM_art_pvals[i,5] <- U_R_art_isa_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
      
      # Urban vs. Rural and Interaction Term
        # dist
        formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_dist*dfe"))
        X_ART_dist <- art(formula, data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),])
        X_aovART_dist <- anova(X_ART_dist)
        Int_art_dist_pval <- X_aovART_dist["urb_cat_dist:dfe","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for the interaction term
        foliar_PM_art_pvals[i,4] <- Int_art_dist_pval # this places it into the matrix we made

        # isa
        formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_isa*dfe"))
        X_ART_isa <- art(formula, data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),])
        X_aovART_isa <- anova(X_ART_isa)
        U_R_art_isa_pval <- X_aovART_isa["urb_cat_isa","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for urban vs. rural
        Int_art_isa_pval <- X_aovART_isa["urb_cat_isa:dfe","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for the interaction term
        foliar_PM_art_pvals[i,6] <- Int_art_isa_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
    
    
    # pairwise comparisons
    
      # isa
      art.con_X_ART_isa <- art.con(X_ART_isa,~urb_cat_isa*dfe, adjust="none")
      art.con_X_ART_isa <- as.data.frame(art.con_X_ART_isa)
      letters_isa <- cldList(
      p.value ~ contrast,
      data = art.con_X_ART_isa,
      threshold = 0.05  
    )
        # placing these results into our matrix
      
          # pairwise comparisons
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,2] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[1]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,3] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[2]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,4] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[3]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,5] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[4]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,6] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[5]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,7] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[6]
          
          # letters
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,8] <- letters_isa$Letter[1]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,9] <- letters_isa$Letter[2]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,10] <- letters_isa$Letter[3]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,11] <- letters_isa$Letter[4]
          
    
      # dist
      art.con_X_ART_dist <- art.con(X_ART_dist,~urb_cat_dist*dfe, adjust="none")
      art.con_X_ART_dist <- as.data.frame(art.con_X_ART_dist)
      letters_dist <- cldList(
      p.value ~ contrast,
      data = art.con_X_ART_dist,
      threshold = 0.05
      )
      
        # placing these results into our matrix
      
          # pairwise comparisons
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,2] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[1]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,3] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[2]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,4] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[3]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,5] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[4]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,6] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[5]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,7] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[6]
          
          # letters
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,8] <- letters_dist$Letter[1]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,9] <- letters_dist$Letter[2]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,10] <- letters_dist$Letter[3]
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,11] <- letters_dist$Letter[4]
    }
        # this is the matrix with p-values! now we make it into a data frame 
        foliar_PM_art_pvals <- as.data.frame(foliar_PM_art_pvals)
        
        # make sure all the numbers are classified as "numeric"
        foliar_PM_art_pvals$Edge_vs_Interior <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pvals$Edge_vs_Interior)
        foliar_PM_art_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_Dist <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_Dist)
        foliar_PM_art_pvals$Interaction_Dist <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pvals$Interaction_Dist)
        foliar_PM_art_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_ISA <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_ISA)
        foliar_PM_art_pvals$Interaction_ISA <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pvals$Interaction_ISA)
        
        # reduce all p-values in the matrix to two decimal points
        foliar_PM_art_pvals[] <- lapply(foliar_PM_art_pvals, function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) signif(x, digits = 2) else x) 
        
        # this is the matrix with p-values! now we make it into a data frame 
        foliar_PM_art_pvals <- as.data.frame(foliar_PM_art_pvals)
        
        # write the data frame to a csv
        write.csv(foliar_PM_art_pvals, file.path(stats_dir, "foliar_PM_art_pvals.csv"), row.names = TRUE)
        
    # the other matrices store pairwise comparisons & letters to indicate significance
        
        # dist
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist <- as.data.frame(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist)
        
          # making sure everything is numeric
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,2] <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,2])
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,3] <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,3])
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,4] <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,4])
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,5] <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,5])
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,6] <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,6])
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,7] <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,7])
            
          # reduce all p-values to two decimal points
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist[] <- lapply(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist, function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) signif(x, digits = 2) else x) 
            
          # write the data frame to a csv
            write.csv(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist, file.path(stats_dir,"foliar_PM_art_pairwise_dist.csv"), row.names = TRUE)
        
        # isa
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa <- as.data.frame(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa)
          foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa <- as.data.frame(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa)
        
          # making sure everything is numeric
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,2] <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,2])
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,3] <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,3])
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,4] <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,4])
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,5] <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,5])
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,6] <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,6])
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,7] <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,7])
            
          # reduce all p-values to two decimal points
            foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa[] <- lapply(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa, function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) signif(x, digits = 2) else x) 
            
          # write the data frame to a csv
            write.csv(foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa, file.path(stats_dir,"foliar_PM_art_pairwise_isa.csv"), row.names = TRUE)
            
            
# Ambient
  
  # create an empty matrix, with a row for each response variable and a column each for: p-value on dfe, p-value for urban vs rural (by dist), p-value for interaction term (by dist), p-value for urban vs rural (by %ISA), p-value for interaction term (by %ISA)
  
      ambient_PM_art_pvals <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=44, ncol=6)
      colnames(ambient_PM_art_pvals) <- c("Element","Edge_vs_Interior","Urb_v_Rural_Dist","Interaction_Dist","Urb_v_Rural_ISA","Interaction_ISA" )
      ambient_PM_art_pvals[,1] <- ambient_element_names
  
  
  # create an empty matrix, with a row for each element and a column for each pairwise comparison
      
      ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=44, ncol=22)
      ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,1] <- ambient_element_names
      colnames(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist) <- 
        c("Element",
          "Rural,0 - Rural,90",
          "Rural,0 - Rural,Open",
          "Rural,0 - Urban,0",
          "Rural,0 - Urban,90",
          "Rural,0 - Urban,Open",
          "Rural,90 - Rural,Open",
          "Rural,90 - Urban,0",
          "Rural,90 - Urban,90",
          "Rural,90 - Urban,Open",
          "Rural,Open - Urban,0",
          "Rural,Open - Urban,90",
          "Rural,Open - Urban,Open",
          "Urban,0 - Urban,90",
          "Urban,0 - Urban,Open",
          "Urban,90 - Urban,Open",
          "Rural0",
          "Rural90",
          "RuralOpen",
          "Urban0",
          "Urban90",
          "UrbanOpen"
          )
      
      ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=44, ncol=22)
      ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,1] <- ambient_element_names
      colnames(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa) <- 
        c("Element",
          "Rural,0 - Rural,90",
          "Rural,0 - Rural,Open",
          "Rural,0 - Urban,0",
          "Rural,0 - Urban,90",
          "Rural,0 - Urban,Open",
          "Rural,90 - Rural,Open",
          "Rural,90 - Urban,0",
          "Rural,90 - Urban,90",
          "Rural,90 - Urban,Open",
          "Rural,Open - Urban,0",
          "Rural,Open - Urban,90",
          "Rural,Open - Urban,Open",
          "Urban,0 - Urban,90",
          "Urban,0 - Urban,Open",
          "Urban,90 - Urban,Open",
          "Rural0",
          "Rural90",
          "RuralOpen",
          "Urban0",
          "Urban90",
          "UrbanOpen"
          )
      
  # now the matrices are ready to go, and we can fill it with these p-values using the loop below
  
  library(ARTool)
  
  for (i in c(1:44)) {
  
  element <- ambient_element_names[i]
  
  no.nas <- AmbientPMCompxUNESites[complete.cases(AmbientPMCompxUNESites[, element]), ]
  
  # ART Anova
      
    # Edge vs. Interior
    formula <- as.formula(paste(ambient_element_names[i], "~ dfe"))
    X_ART_EvI <- art(formula, data = no.nas)
    X_aovART_EvI <- anova(X_ART_EvI)
    X_aovART_EvI_pval <- X_aovART_EvI["dfe","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for edge vs. interior
    ambient_PM_art_pvals[i,2] <- X_aovART_EvI_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
    
    # Urban vs Rural
      # dist
      formula <- as.formula(paste(ambient_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_dist"))
      X_ART_dist <- art(formula, data = no.nas)
      X_aovART_dist <- anova(X_ART_dist)
      U_R_art_dist_pval <- X_aovART_dist["urb_cat_dist","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for urban vs. rural
      ambient_PM_art_pvals[i,3] <- U_R_art_dist_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
      
      # isa
      formula <- as.formula(paste(ambient_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_isa"))
      X_ART_isa <- art(formula, data = no.nas)
      X_aovART_isa <- anova(X_ART_isa)
      U_R_art_isa_pval <- X_aovART_isa["urb_cat_isa","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for urban vs. rural
      ambient_PM_art_pvals[i,5] <- U_R_art_isa_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
      
    # Urban vs. Rural and Interaction Term
      # dist
      formula <- as.formula(paste(ambient_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_dist*dfe"))
      X_ART_dist <- art(formula, data = no.nas)
      X_aovART_dist <- anova(X_ART_dist)
      Int_art_dist_pval <- X_aovART_dist["urb_cat_dist:dfe","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for the interaction term
      ambient_PM_art_pvals[i,4] <- Int_art_dist_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
    
    # Urban vs. Rural and Interaction Term (by %ISA)
      # isa
      formula <- as.formula(paste(ambient_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_isa*dfe"))
      X_ART_isa <- art(formula, data = no.nas)
      X_aovART_isa <- anova(X_ART_isa)
      Int_art_isa_pval <- X_aovART_isa["urb_cat_isa:dfe","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for the interaction term
      ambient_PM_art_pvals[i,6] <- Int_art_isa_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
  
  # pairwise comparisons
  
    # isa
    art.con_X_ART_isa <- art.con(X_ART_isa,~urb_cat_isa*dfe, adjust="none")
    art.con_X_ART_isa <- as.data.frame(art.con_X_ART_isa)
    letters_isa <- cldList(
    p.value ~ contrast,
    data = art.con_X_ART_isa,
    threshold = 0.05  
  )
      # placing these results into our matrix
    
        # pairwise comparisons
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,2] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[1]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,3] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[2]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,4] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[3]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,5] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[4]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,6] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[5]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,7] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[6]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,8] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[7]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,9] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[8]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,10] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[9]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,11] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[10]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,12] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[11]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,13] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[12]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,14] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[13]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,15] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[14]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,16] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[15]
        
        # letters
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,17] <- letters_isa$Letter[1]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,18] <- letters_isa$Letter[2]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,19] <- letters_isa$Letter[3]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,20] <- letters_isa$Letter[4]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,21] <- letters_isa$Letter[5]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[i,22] <- letters_isa$Letter[6]
        
  
    # dist
    art.con_X_ART_dist <- art.con(X_ART_dist,~urb_cat_dist*dfe, adjust="none")
    art.con_X_ART_dist <- as.data.frame(art.con_X_ART_dist)
    letters_dist <- cldList(
    p.value ~ contrast,
    data = art.con_X_ART_dist,
    threshold = 0.05
    )
    
      # placing these results into our matrix
    
        # pairwise comparisons
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,2] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[1]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,3] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[2]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,4] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[3]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,5] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[4]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,6] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[5]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,7] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[6]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,8] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[7]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,9] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[8]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,10] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[9]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,11] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[10]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,12] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[11]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,13] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[12]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,14] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[13]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,15] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[14]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,16] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[15]
        
        # letters
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,17] <- letters_dist$Letter[1]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,18] <- letters_dist$Letter[2]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,19] <- letters_dist$Letter[3]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,20] <- letters_dist$Letter[4]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,21] <- letters_dist$Letter[5]
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[i,22] <- letters_dist$Letter[6]
  
  }
  
  # this is the matrix with p-values! now we make it into a data frame 
      ambient_PM_art_pvals <- as.data.frame(ambient_PM_art_pvals)
      
      # make sure all the numbers are classified as "numeric"
      ambient_PM_art_pvals$Edge_vs_Interior <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pvals$Edge_vs_Interior)
      ambient_PM_art_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_Dist <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_Dist)
      ambient_PM_art_pvals$Interaction_Dist <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pvals$Interaction_Dist)
      ambient_PM_art_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_ISA <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_ISA)
      ambient_PM_art_pvals$Interaction_ISA <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pvals$Interaction_ISA)
      
      # reduce all p-values in the matrix to two decimal points
      ambient_PM_art_pvals[] <- lapply(ambient_PM_art_pvals, function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) signif(x, digits = 2) else x) 
      
      # write the data frame to a csv
      write.csv(ambient_PM_art_pvals, file.path(stats_dir,"ambient_PM_art_pvals.csv"), row.names = TRUE)
      
  # the other matrices store pairwise comparisons & letters to indicate significance
      
      # dist
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist <- as.data.frame(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist)
      
        # making sure everything is numeric
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,2] <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,2])
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,3] <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,3])
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,4] <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,4])
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,5] <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,5])
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,6] <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,6])
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,7] <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[,7])
          
        # reduce all p-values to two decimal points
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist[] <- lapply(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist, function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) signif(x, digits = 2) else x) 
          
        # write the data frame to a csv
          write.csv(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist, file.path(stats_dir,"ambient_PM_art_pairwise_dist.csv"), row.names = TRUE)
      
      # isa
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa <- as.data.frame(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa)
        ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa <- as.data.frame(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa)
      
        # making sure everything is numeric
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,2] <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,2])
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,3] <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,3])
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,4] <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,4])
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,5] <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,5])
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,6] <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,6])
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,7] <- as.numeric(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[,7])
          
        # reduce all p-values to two decimal points
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa[] <- lapply(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa, function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) signif(x, digits = 2) else x) 
          ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa
          
        # write the data frame to a csv
          write.csv(ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa, file.path(stats_dir,"ambient_PM_art_pairwise_isa.csv"), row.names = TRUE)
    
```
##### ART ANOVA (NO open sites for ambient)
```{r}
# Foliar
    
    # create an empty matrix, with a row for each element and a column each for: p-value on edge vs. interior, p-value for urban vs rural (by dist), p-value for interaction term (by dist), p-value for urban vs rural (by %ISA), p-value for interaction term (by %ISA)
    
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=6)
        colnames(foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals) <- c("Element","Edge_vs_Interior","Urb_v_Rural_Dist","Interaction_Dist","Urb_v_Rural_ISA","Interaction_ISA" )
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals[,1] <- all_element_names
        
    # create an empty matrix, with a row for each element and a column for each pairwise comparison
        
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=11)
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Rural,0 - Rural,90",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,90",
            "Urban,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural0",
            "Rural90",
            "Urban0",
            "Urban90"
            )
        
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=11)
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Rural,0 - Rural,90",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,90",
            "Urban,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural0",
            "Rural90",
            "Urban0",
            "Urban90"
            )
        
    # now the matrices are ready to go, and we can fill it with these p-values using the loop below
    
    for (i in c(1:26)) {
      
    element <- all_element_names[i]
    no.nas <- AmbientPMCompxUNESites[complete.cases(AmbientPMCompxUNESites[, element]), ]
    

    
    # ART Anova
        
      # Edge vs. Interior
      formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~ dfe"))
      X_ART_EvI <- art(formula, data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),])
      X_aovART_EvI <- anova(X_ART_EvI)
      X_aovART_EvI_pval <- X_aovART_EvI["dfe","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for edge vs. interior
      foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals[i,2] <- X_aovART_EvI_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
      
      # Urban vs. Rural
        # dist
        formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_dist"))
        X_ART_dist <- art(formula, data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),])
        X_aovART_dist <- anova(X_ART_dist)
        U_R_art_dist_pval <- X_aovART_dist["urb_cat_dist","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for urban vs. rural
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals[i,3] <- U_R_art_dist_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
        
        # isa
        formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_isa"))
        X_ART_isa <- art(formula, data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),])
        X_aovART_isa <- anova(X_ART_isa)
        U_R_art_isa_pval <- X_aovART_isa["urb_cat_isa","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for urban vs. rural
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals[i,5] <- U_R_art_isa_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
      
      # Urban vs. Rural and Interaction Term
        # dist
        formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_dist*dfe"))
        X_ART_dist <- art(formula, data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),])
        X_aovART_dist <- anova(X_ART_dist)
        Int_art_dist_pval <- X_aovART_dist["urb_cat_dist:dfe","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for the interaction term
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals[i,4] <- Int_art_dist_pval # this places it into the matrix we made

        # isa
        formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_isa*dfe"))
        X_ART_isa <- art(formula, data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),])
        X_aovART_isa <- anova(X_ART_isa)
        U_R_art_isa_pval <- X_aovART_isa["urb_cat_isa","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for urban vs. rural
        Int_art_isa_pval <- X_aovART_isa["urb_cat_isa:dfe","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for the interaction term
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals[i,6] <- Int_art_isa_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
    
    
    # pairwise comparisons
    
      # isa
      art.con_X_ART_isa <- art.con(X_ART_isa,~urb_cat_isa*dfe, adjust="none")
      art.con_X_ART_isa <- as.data.frame(art.con_X_ART_isa)
      letters_isa <- cldList(
      p.value ~ contrast,
      data = art.con_X_ART_isa,
      threshold = 0.05  
    )
        # placing these results into our matrix
      
          # pairwise comparisons
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[i,2] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[1]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[i,3] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[2]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[i,4] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[3]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[i,5] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[4]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[i,6] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[5]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[i,7] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[6]
          
          # letters
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[i,8] <- letters_isa$Letter[1]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[i,9] <- letters_isa$Letter[2]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[i,10] <- letters_isa$Letter[3]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[i,11] <- letters_isa$Letter[4]
          
    
      # dist
      art.con_X_ART_dist <- art.con(X_ART_dist,~urb_cat_dist*dfe, adjust="none")
      art.con_X_ART_dist <- as.data.frame(art.con_X_ART_dist)
      letters_dist <- cldList(
      p.value ~ contrast,
      data = art.con_X_ART_dist,
      threshold = 0.05
      )
      
        # placing these results into our matrix
      
          # pairwise comparisons
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist[i,2] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[1]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist[i,3] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[2]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist[i,4] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[3]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist[i,5] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[4]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist[i,6] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[5]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist[i,7] <- art.con_X_ART_dist$p.value[6]
          
          # letters
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist[i,8] <- letters_dist$Letter[1]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist[i,9] <- letters_dist$Letter[2]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist[i,10] <- letters_dist$Letter[3]
          foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist[i,11] <- letters_dist$Letter[4]
    }
        # this is the matrix with p-values! now we make it into a data frame 
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals <- as.data.frame(foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals)
        
        # make sure all the numbers are classified as "numeric"
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals$Edge_vs_Interior <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals$Edge_vs_Interior)
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_Dist <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_Dist)
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals$Interaction_Dist <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals$Interaction_Dist)
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_ISA <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals$Urb_v_Rural_ISA)
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals$Interaction_ISA <- as.numeric(foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals$Interaction_ISA)
        
        # reduce all p-values in the matrix to two decimal points
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals[] <- lapply(foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals, function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) signif(x, digits = 2) else x) 
        
        # this is the matrix with p-values! now we make it into a data frame 
        foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals <- as.data.frame(foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals)
        
        # write the data frame to a csv
        write.csv(foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals, file.path(stats_dir,"foliar_PM_art_NOopen_pvals.csv"), row.names = TRUE)
        
    # the other matrices store pairwise comparisons & letters to indicate significance

# Ambient
            
  # subsetting just the edge & interior sites
  AllCompxUNESites_NOopen <- subset(AllCompxUNESites, dfe=="0"|dfe=="90")
  
  # create an empty matrix, with a row for each response variable and a column each for: p-value on dfe, p-value for urban vs rural (by dist), p-value for interaction term (by dist), p-value for urban vs rural (by %ISA), p-value for interaction term (by %ISA)
  
      ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pvals <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=6)
      colnames(ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pvals) <- c("Element","Edge_vs_Interior","Urb_v_Rural_Dist","Interaction_Dist","Urb_v_Rural_ISA","Interaction_ISA" )
      ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pvals[,1] <- all_element_names
  
  
  # create an empty matrix, with a row for each element and a column for each pairwise comparison
      
      ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=11)
      ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist[,1] <- all_element_names
      colnames(ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_dist) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Rural,0 - Rural,90",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,90",
            "Urban,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural0",
            "Rural90",
            "Urban0",
            "Urban90"
            )
      
      ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=11)
      ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[,1] <- all_element_names
      colnames(ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Rural,0 - Rural,90",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,90",
            "Urban,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural0",
            "Rural90",
            "Urban0",
            "Urban90"
            )
      
  # now the matrices are ready to go, and we can fill it with these p-values using the loop below
  
  library(ARTool)
  
  for (i in c(1:26)) {
  
  element <- all_element_names[i]
  
  no.nas <- AllCompxUNESites_NOopen[complete.cases(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen[, element]), ]
  
  # ART Anova
      
    # Edge vs. Interior
    formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~ dfe"))
    X_ART_EvI <- art(formula, data = no.nas)
    X_aovART_EvI <- anova(X_ART_EvI)
    X_aovART_EvI_pval <- X_aovART_EvI["dfe","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for edge vs. interior
    ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pvals[i,2] <- X_aovART_EvI_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
    
    # Urban vs Rural
      # dist
      formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_dist"))
      X_ART_dist <- art(formula, data = no.nas)
      X_aovART_dist <- anova(X_ART_dist)
      U_R_art_dist_pval <- X_aovART_dist["urb_cat_dist","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for urban vs. rural
      ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pvals[i,3] <- U_R_art_dist_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
      
      # isa
      formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_isa"))
      X_ART_isa <- art(formula, data = no.nas)
      X_aovART_isa <- anova(X_ART_isa)
      U_R_art_isa_pval <- X_aovART_isa["urb_cat_isa","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for urban vs. rural
      ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pvals[i,5] <- U_R_art_isa_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
      
    # Urban vs. Rural and Interaction Term
      # dist
      formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_dist*dfe"))
      X_ART_dist <- art(formula, data = no.nas)
      X_aovART_dist <- anova(X_ART_dist)
      Int_art_dist_pval <- X_aovART_dist["urb_cat_dist:dfe","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for the interaction term
      ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pvals[i,4] <- Int_art_dist_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
    
    # Urban vs. Rural and Interaction Term (by %ISA)
      # isa
      formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_isa*dfe"))
      X_ART_isa <- art(formula, data = no.nas)
      X_aovART_isa <- anova(X_ART_isa)
      Int_art_isa_pval <- X_aovART_isa["urb_cat_isa:dfe","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for the interaction term
      ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pvals[i,6] <- Int_art_isa_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
  
  # pairwise comparisons
  
    # isa
    art.con_X_ART_isa <- art.con(X_ART_isa,~urb_cat_isa*dfe, adjust="none")
    art.con_X_ART_isa <- as.data.frame(art.con_X_ART_isa)
    letters_isa <- cldList(
    p.value ~ contrast,
    data = art.con_X_ART_isa,
    threshold = 0.05  
  )
      # placing these results into our matrix
    
        # pairwise comparisons

        #ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[i,3] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[2]
        #ambient_PM_art_NOopen_pairwise_isa[i,4] <- art.con_X_ART_isa$p.value[3]

```
##### Kruskal-Wallis Test (does not include open sites)
```{r}

# creating a column with the groups "Rural0", "Rural90", "Urban0", and "Urban90"
 FoliarPMCompxUNESites$groupISA <- paste(FoliarPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_isa,FoliarPMCompxUNESites$dfe)
 FoliarPMCompxUNESites$groupDist <- paste(FoliarPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_dist,FoliarPMCompxUNESites$dfe)
 
 AmbientPMCompxUNESites$groupISA <- paste(AmbientPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_isa,AmbientPMCompxUNESites$dfe)
 AmbientPMCompxUNESites$groupDist <- paste(AmbientPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_dist,AmbientPMCompxUNESites$dfe)

# Foliar

    # create an empty matrix, with a row for each element and a column for each pairwise comparison
        
        foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=12)
        foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Overall pval",
            "Rural,0 - Rural,90",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,90",
            "Urban,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural0",
            "Rural90",
            "Urban0",
            "Urban90"
            )
        
        foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=12)
        foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Overall pval",
            "Rural,0 - Rural,90",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,90",
            "Urban,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural0",
            "Rural90",
            "Urban0",
            "Urban90"
            )
        
for (i in c(1:26)) {
  
          element <- all_element_names[i] 
          
          
# pairwise comparisons
          
    # ISA
          
          # Kruskal-Wallis Test
          formula <- as.formula(paste(element, "~groupISA"))
          X_kw_AvF <- kruskal.test(formula, data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),])
        
          # Dunn post-hoc pairwise
          dunn_res <- dunn_test(
                data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),],
                formula,
                p.adjust.method = "BH"
              )
         
          # Create named vector of adjusted p-values
          pvals <- dunn_res$p.adj
          names(pvals) <- paste(dunn_res$group1, dunn_res$group2, sep = "-")
          
          # Generate letters
          letters <- multcompLetters(pvals)$Letters
          letters <- as.data.frame(letters)
              
              # placing these results into our matrix
            
                # pairwise comparisons
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,2] <- X_kw_AvF$p.value
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,3] <- dunn_res$p[1]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,4] <- dunn_res$p[2]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,5] <- dunn_res$p[3]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,6] <- dunn_res$p[4]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,7] <- dunn_res$p[5]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,8] <- dunn_res$p[6]
                
                # letters
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,9] <- letters$letters[1]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,10] <- letters$letters[2]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,11] <- letters$letters[3]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,12] <- letters$letters[4]
                
      # Dist
          # Kruskal-Wallis Test
          formula <- as.formula(paste(element, "~groupDist"))
          X_kw_AvF <- kruskal.test(formula, data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),])
        
          # Dunn post-hoc pairwise
          dunn_res <- dunn_test(
                data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(FoliarPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),],
                formula,
                p.adjust.method = "BH"
              )
         
          # Create named vector of adjusted p-values
          pvals <- dunn_res$p.adj
          names(pvals) <- paste(dunn_res$group1, dunn_res$group2, sep = "-")
          
          # Generate letters
          letters <- multcompLetters(pvals)$Letters
          letters <- as.data.frame(letters)
              
              # placing these results into our matrix
            
                # pairwise comparisons
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,2] <- X_kw_AvF$p.value
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,3] <- dunn_res$p[1]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,4] <- dunn_res$p[2]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,5] <- dunn_res$p[3]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,6] <- dunn_res$p[4]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,7] <- dunn_res$p[5]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,8] <- dunn_res$p[6]
                
                # letters
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,9] <- letters$letters[1]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,10] <- letters$letters[2]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,11] <- letters$letters[3]
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,12] <- letters$letters[4]
                
}
            # making sure everything is numeric
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa <- as.data.frame(foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa)
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa <- foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa %>%
                      mutate(across(c(2:8), as.numeric))
                
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist <- as.data.frame(foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist)
                foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist <- foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist %>%
                      mutate(across(c(2:8), as.numeric))
            
          # reduce all p-values to two decimal points
            foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[] <- lapply(foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa, function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) signif(x, digits = 2) else x) 
            foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[] <- lapply(foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist, function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) signif(x, digits = 2) else x) 
            
          # write the data frame to a csv
            write.csv(foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa, file.path(stats_dir,"foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_isa.csv"), row.names = TRUE)
            write.csv(foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist, file.path(stats_dir,"foliar_PM_kw_pairwise_dist.csv"), row.names = TRUE)

# Ambient

    # create an empty matrix, with a row for each element and a column for each pairwise comparison
        
        ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_dist <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=23)
        ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[,1] <- all_element_names

          c("Element",

            "Rural0",
            "Rural90",
            "RuralOpen",
            "Urban0",

          )
        
        
        ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_isa <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=23)

        colnames(ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_isa) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Overall pval",
            "Rural,0 - Rural,90",
            "Rural,0 - Rural,Open",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,90",
            "Rural,0 - Urban,Open",
            "Rural,90 - Rural,Open",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,0",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,90",
            "Rural,90 - Urban,Open",
            "Rural,Open - Urban,0",
            "Rural,Open - Urban,90",
            "Rural,Open - Urban,Open",
            "Urban,0 - Urban,90",

              # Dunn post-hoc pairwise

                    data = AmbientPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(AmbientPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),],
                    formula,
                    p.adjust.method = "BH"
                  )
             
              # Create named vector of adjusted p-values

                  # placing these results into our matrix
                
                    # pairwise comparisons

                    ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,3] <- dunn_res$p[1]
                    ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,4] <- dunn_res$p[2]

                    ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,20] <- letters$letters[3]

                    ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,22] <- letters$letters[5]
                    ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_isa[i,23] <- letters$letters[6]
                    
          # Dist
              # Kruskal-Wallis Test
              formula <- as.formula(paste(element, "~groupDist"))
              X_kw_AvF <- kruskal.test(formula, data = AmbientPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(AmbientPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),])
            
              # Dunn post-hoc pairwise
              dunn_res <- dunn_test(
                    data = AmbientPMCompxUNESites[!is.na(AmbientPMCompxUNESites[[element]]),],
                    formula,
                    p.adjust.method = "BH"
                  )
             
              # Create named vector of adjusted p-values
              pvals <- dunn_res$p.adj
              names(pvals) <- paste(dunn_res$group1, dunn_res$group2, sep = "-")
              
              # Generate letters
              letters <- multcompLetters(pvals)$Letters
              letters <- as.data.frame(letters)
                  
                  # placing these results into our matrix
                
                    # pairwise comparisons
                    ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,2] <- X_kw_AvF$p.value
                    ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,3] <- dunn_res$p[1]
                    ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,4] <- dunn_res$p[2]

                    # letters

                    ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,20] <- letters$letters[3]

                    ambient_PM_kw_pairwise_dist[i,22] <- letters$letters[5]

    }
                # making sure everything is numeric
```

#### Testing Pairwise Comparisons ART ANOVA
```{r, eval=FALSE}
# Cu
    # because the pairwise comparisons for the Cu graph look dubious (much higher mean is not reading as significantly different) I am going to walk through the ART ANOVA for Cu
    Cu_ART <- art(Cu~urb_cat_dist*dfe, data = FoliarPMCompxUNESites)
    Cu_aovART <- anova(Cu_ART)
    
    art.con_Cu_ART <- art.con(Cu_ART,~urb_cat_dist*dfe, adjust="none")
    art.con_Cu_ART <- as.data.frame(art.con_Cu_ART)
      letters <- cldList(
      p.value ~ contrast,
      data = art.con_Cu_ART,
      threshold = 0.05  
    )
    
    # now on just the rural samples
      
    CuTest_Rural <- subset(FoliarPMCompxUNESites,urb_cat_dist=="Rural")  
    Cu_ART_Rural <- art(Cu~dfe, data = CuTest_Rural)
    Cu_aovART_Rural <- anova(Cu_ART_Rural)
    art.con_Cu_ART <- art.con(Cu_ART_Rural,~dfe, adjust="none")
    art.con_Cu_ART <- as.data.frame(art.con_Cu_ART)
      letters <- cldList(
      p.value ~ contrast,
      data = art.con_Cu_ART,
      threshold = 0.05  
    )
      
    # summarizing the rural group
    summary <- cbind(aggregate(FoliarPMCompxUNESites$Cu,                           list(FoliarPMCompxUNESites$dfe,FoliarPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_dist),FUN=mean),
                     aggregate(FoliarPMCompxUNESites$Cu,                            list(FoliarPMCompxUNESites$dfe,FoliarPMCompxUNESites$urb_cat_dist),FUN=stderr))
    summary <- summary[,c(1:3,6)]
    colnames(summary) <- c("dfe","urb_cat_isa","perc_comp","se")
    
# Cr

# because some of the significance letters on foliar Cr look dubious, I will look at the pairwise comparisons for the groups.

```


### Ambient vs. Foliar  
#### Including Open Sites (ART ANOVA only, not used)
```{r,eval=FALSE}

# create an empty matrix, with a row for each response variable and a column each for: p-value on ambient vs foliar, p-value for interaction of urbanization w ambient vs foliar  (by %ISA), p-value for interaction of urbanization w ambient vs foliar  (by distance to Boston)

    all_PM_art_pvals <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=4)
    colnames(all_PM_art_pvals) <- c("Element",
                                    "AvF",
                                    "Interaction_UvR_AvF_ISA",
                                    "Interaction_UvR_AvF_dist"
                                    )
    all_PM_art_pvals[,1] <- all_element_names

    
# now the matrices are ready to go, and we can fill it with these p-values using the loop below

library(ARTool)

for (i in c(1:26)) {
  

element <- all_element_names[i]  

# ART Anova
    
  # Ambient vs. Foliar
    formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~particle_source"))
    X_ART_AvF <- art(formula, data = AllCompxUNESites)
    X_aovART_AvF <- anova(X_ART_AvF)
    X_aovART_AvF_pval <- X_aovART_AvF["particle_source","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for ambient vs. foliar
    all_PM_art_pvals[i,2] <- X_aovART_AvF_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
  
  # EvI vs AvF Interaction
    
    # It's not possible to run this ANOVA with the open site data included, because there is a group 
    # that doesn't exist (dfe = "Open" + particle_source = "Foliar"). 
    
  # UvR vs. AvF Interaction 
    
    # ISA
    formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_isa*particle_source"))
    X_ART_UvR_AvF <- art(formula, data = AllCompxUNESites)
    X_aovART_UvR_AvF <- anova(X_ART_UvR_AvF)
    UvR_AvF_art_pval <- X_aovART_UvR_AvF["urb_cat_isa:particle_source","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for the interaction term
    all_PM_art_pvals[i,3] <- UvR_AvF_art_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
    
    # Dist
    formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~urb_cat_dist*particle_source"))
    X_ART_UvR_AvF <- art(formula, data = AllCompxUNESites)
    X_aovART_UvR_AvF <- anova(X_ART_UvR_AvF)
    UvR_AvF_art_pval <- X_aovART_UvR_AvF["urb_cat_dist:particle_source","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value for the interaction term
    all_PM_art_pvals[i,4] <- UvR_AvF_art_pval # this places it into the matrix we made  
    }

# this is the matrix with p-values! now we make it into a data frame 
    all_PM_art_pvals <- as.data.frame(all_PM_art_pvals)
    
    # make sure all the numbers are classified as "numeric"
    all_PM_art_pvals$AvF <- as.numeric(all_PM_art_pvals$AvF)
    all_PM_art_pvals$Interaction_UvR_AvF_ISA <- as.numeric(all_PM_art_pvals$Interaction_UvR_AvF_ISA)
    all_PM_art_pvals$Interaction_UvR_AvF_dist <- as.numeric(all_PM_art_pvals$Interaction_UvR_AvF_dist)
    
    # reduce all p-values in the matrix to two decimal points
    all_PM_art_pvals[] <- lapply(all_PM_art_pvals, function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) signif(x, digits = 2) else x) 
    
    # write the data frame to a csv
    write.csv(all_PM_art_pvals, file.path(stats_dir,"all_PM_art_pvals.csv"), row.names = TRUE)
    
# Individual: Traffic-associated elements
    # by urb_cat_isa and ambient vs foliar
      traffic_ART_isa <- art(traffic~urb_cat_isa*particle_source, data = AllCompxUNESites)    
      traffic_ART_isa_aov <- anova(traffic_ART_isa)    
      summary(traffic_ART_isa_aov)
```

#### Without Open Sites
```{r}
# running a one-way ART ANOVA & pairwise comparisons

# subsetting just the edge & interior sites
  AllCompxUNESites_NOopen <- subset(AllCompxUNESites, dfe=="0"|dfe=="90")
  write.csv(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen,file.path(data_dir,"AllCompxUNESites_NOopen.csv"),row.names=FALSE)


  # making matrix to store the pairwise data
      allPM_art_pairwise <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=4)
      allPM_art_pairwise[,1] <- all_element_names
      colnames(allPM_art_pairwise) <- 
        c("Element",
          "Ambient - Foliar",
          "Ambient",
          "Foliar"
          )
      
    # running a loop to fill this matrix with p-values and pairwise comparisons for each element
      
        for (i in c(1:26)) {
          
          element <- all_element_names[i]  
    
        # ART Anova
          formula <- as.formula(paste(element, "~ particle_source"))
          X_ART_AvF <- art(formula, data = AllCompxUNESites_NOopen[!is.na(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen[[element]]),])
          X_aovART_AvF <- anova(X_ART_AvF)
        
        # pairwise comparisons
            art.con_X_ART <- art.con(X_ART_AvF,~particle_source, adjust="none")
            art.con_X_ART <- as.data.frame(art.con_X_ART)
            letters <- cldList(
            p.value ~ contrast,
            data = art.con_X_ART,
            threshold = 0.05  )
              
              # placing these results into our matrix
            
                # pairwise comparisons
                allPM_art_pairwise[i,2] <- art.con_X_ART$p.value[1]
                
                # letters
                allPM_art_pairwise[i,3] <- letters$Letter[1]
                allPM_art_pairwise[i,4] <- letters$Letter[2]

        }
      
      allPM_art_pairwise <- as.data.frame(allPM_art_pairwise)
      allPM_art_pairwise[,2] <- as.numeric(allPM_art_pairwise[,2])
      allPM_art_pairwise[] <- lapply(allPM_art_pairwise, function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) signif(x, digits = 2) else x)
      
# running a kruskal-wallis one-way test
      
      # making matrix to store the pairwise data
      allPM_kw_pairwise <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=4)
      allPM_kw_pairwise[,1] <- all_element_names
      colnames(allPM_kw_pairwise) <- 
        c("Element",
          "Ambient - Foliar",
          "Ambient",
          "Foliar"
          )
      
    # running a loop to fill this matrix with p-values and pairwise comparisons for each element
      
        for (i in c(1:26)) {
          element <- all_element_names[i]  
    
        # Kruskal-Wallis Test
          formula <- as.formula(paste(element, "~particle_source"))
          X_kw_AvF <- kruskal.test(formula, data = AllCompxUNESites_NOopen)
        
        # Wilcox post-hoc pairwise
         w <- wilcox.test(formula,
            data = AllCompxUNESites_NOopen,
            exact = FALSE)
         
         alpha <- 0.05
          letters <- data.frame(
            particle_source = levels(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen$particle_source),
            letters = if (w$p.value < alpha) c("a", "b") else c("a", "a")
          )
          
          letters
              
              # placing these results into our matrix
            
                # pairwise comparisons
                allPM_kw_pairwise[i,2] <- X_kw_AvF$p.value
                
                # letters
                allPM_kw_pairwise[i,3] <- letters$letters[1]
                allPM_kw_pairwise[i,4] <- letters$letters[2]

        }
      
      allPM_kw_pairwise <- as.data.frame(allPM_kw_pairwise)
      allPM_kw_pairwise[,2] <- as.numeric(allPM_kw_pairwise[,2])
      allPM_kw_pairwise[] <- lapply(allPM_kw_pairwise, function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) signif(x, digits = 2) else x)
```

##### Sanity check
```{r, eval=FALSE}
      
 # looking at the ranks for traffic, because the means are quite similar but both kruskal-wallis and ART ANOVA show a difference

  # kruskal-wallis      
        AllCompxUNESites_NOopen %>%
        mutate(rank = rank(traffic)) %>%
        group_by(particle_source) %>%
        summarise(mean_rank = mean(rank),
                  median_rank = median(rank),
                  n = n())     
  # ART ANOVA
        m <- art(traffic ~ particle_source, data = AllCompxUNESites_NOopen)
        art_data <- m$aligned.ranks
        head(art_data)
        AllCompxUNESites_NOopen$rank_particle_source <- art_data$particle_source
        aggregate(rank_particle_source ~ particle_source, data = AllCompxUNESites_NOopen, mean)
        
        
# visualizing the raw data
        
  ggplot(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen, aes(particle_source, traffic)) +
  geom_violin() +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.2)
  
  
# visualizing the ranks (Kruskal-Wallis)
  
  AllCompxUNESites_NOopen$rank_traffic <- rank(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen$traffic, ties.method = "average")
  ggplot(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen, aes(x = particle_source, y = rank_traffic, color = particle_source)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.05, outlier.shape = NA) +
  labs(y = "Rank of traffic")
  
# anova on ranks
  aov <- aov(rank_traffic~particle_source,data=AllCompxUNESites_NOopen)
  summary(aov)
  
# medians
  aggregate(traffic ~ particle_source, data = AllCompxUNESites_NOopen, median)
  
```

##### One-way Pairwise Comparisons (capital letters)
###### ART ANOVA
```{r}

# subsetting just the edge & interior sites
  AllCompxUNESites_NOopen <- subset(AllCompxUNESites, dfe=="0"|dfe=="90")

# subsetting just the edge & interior sites


  ruraledge_isa <- subset(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen, dfe=="0"|urb_cat_isa=="Rural")
  ruralinterior_isa <- subset(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen, dfe=="90"|urb_cat_isa=="Rural")
  urbanedge_isa <- subset(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen, dfe=="90"|urb_cat_isa=="Urban")
  urbaninterior_isa <- subset(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen, dfe=="90"|urb_cat_isa=="Urban")
  
  ruraledge_dist <- subset(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen, dfe=="0"|urb_cat_dist=="Rural")
  ruralinterior_dist <- subset(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen, dfe=="90"|urb_cat_dist=="Rural")
  urbanedge_dist <- subset(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen, dfe=="90"|urb_cat_dist=="Urban")
  urbaninterior_dist <- subset(AllCompxUNESites_NOopen, dfe=="90"|urb_cat_dist=="Urban")

    
# create an empty matrix, with a row for each element and a column for p-values comparing ambient & foliar
  
  # ISA
      allPM_ruraledge_isa_art_pvals <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=2)
        colnames(allPM_ruraledge_isa_art_pvals) <-
          c("Element",
            "Ambient_v_Foliar" )

        allPM_ruralinterior_isa_art_pvals <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=2)
        colnames(allPM_ruralinterior_isa_art_pvals) <-
          c("Element",

        allPM_ruralinterior_isa_art_pvals[,1] <- all_element_names
        
       allPM_urbanedge_isa_art_pvals <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=2)

    # Dist
        allPM_ruraledge_dist_art_pvals <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=2)

          c("Element",
            "Ambient_v_Foliar" )
        allPM_ruraledge_dist_art_pvals[,1] <- all_element_names
        
        allPM_ruralinterior_dist_art_pvals <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=2)

          c("Element",
            "Ambient_v_Foliar" )
        allPM_ruralinterior_dist_art_pvals[,1] <- all_element_names
        
       allPM_urbanedge_dist_art_pvals <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=2)

        allPM_urbaninterior_dist_art_pvals[,1] <- all_element_names
           
# create an empty matrix, with a row for each element and a column for each pairwise comparison
      
      # ISA
        
        allPM_ruraledge_isa_art_pairwise <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=4)
        allPM_ruraledge_isa_art_pairwise[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(allPM_ruraledge_isa_art_pairwise) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Ambient - Foliar",
            "Ambient",
            "Foliar"
            )
        
        allPM_ruralinterior_isa_art_pairwise <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=4)
        allPM_ruralinterior_isa_art_pairwise[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(allPM_ruralinterior_isa_art_pairwise) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Ambient - Foliar",
            "Ambient",
            "Foliar"
            )
        
        allPM_urbanedge_isa_art_pairwise <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=4)
        allPM_urbanedge_isa_art_pairwise[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(allPM_urbanedge_isa_art_pairwise) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Ambient - Foliar",
            "Ambient",
            "Foliar"
            )
        
        allPM_urbaninterior_isa_art_pairwise <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=4)
        allPM_urbaninterior_isa_art_pairwise[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(allPM_urbaninterior_isa_art_pairwise) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Ambient - Foliar",
            "Ambient",
            "Foliar"
            )
        
      # Dist
        
        allPM_ruraledge_dist_art_pairwise <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=4)
        allPM_ruraledge_dist_art_pairwise[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(allPM_ruraledge_dist_art_pairwise) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Ambient - Foliar",
            "Ambient",
            "Foliar"
            )
        
        allPM_ruralinterior_dist_art_pairwise <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=4)
        allPM_ruralinterior_dist_art_pairwise[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(allPM_ruralinterior_dist_art_pairwise) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Ambient - Foliar",
            "Ambient",
            "Foliar"
            )
        
        allPM_urbanedge_dist_art_pairwise <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=4)
        allPM_urbanedge_dist_art_pairwise[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(allPM_urbanedge_dist_art_pairwise) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Ambient - Foliar",
            "Ambient",
            "Foliar"
            )
        
        allPM_urbaninterior_dist_art_pairwise <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=26, ncol=4)
        allPM_urbaninterior_dist_art_pairwise[,1] <- all_element_names
        colnames(allPM_urbaninterior_dist_art_pairwise) <- 
          c("Element",
            "Ambient - Foliar",
            "Ambient",
            "Foliar"
            )
        
    for (i in c(1:26)) {
      
    
    element <- all_element_names[i]  

# ISA  
    
    # Rural Edge
        
        # ART Anova
          formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~ particle_source"))
          X_ART_AvF <- art(formula, data = ruraledge_isa[!is.na(ruraledge_isa[[element]]),])
          X_aovART_AvF <- anova(X_ART_AvF)
          X_aovART_AvF_pval <- X_aovART_AvF["particle_source","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value
          allPM_ruraledge_isa_art_pvals[i,2] <- X_aovART_AvF_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
        
        # pairwise comparisons
            art.con_X_ART <- art.con(X_ART_AvF,~particle_source, adjust="none")
            art.con_X_ART <- as.data.frame(art.con_X_ART)
            letters <- cldList(
            p.value ~ contrast,
            data = art.con_X_ART,
            threshold = 0.05  )
              
              # placing these results into our matrix
            
                # pairwise comparisons
                allPM_ruraledge_isa_art_pairwise[i,2] <- art.con_X_ART$p.value[1]
                
                # letters
                allPM_ruraledge_isa_art_pairwise[i,3] <- letters$Letter[1]
                allPM_ruraledge_isa_art_pairwise[i,4] <- letters$Letter[2]
    
    # Rural Interior
        
        # ART Anova
          formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~ particle_source"))
          X_ART_AvF <- art(formula, data = ruralinterior_isa[!is.na(ruralinterior_isa[[element]]),])
          X_aovART_AvF <- anova(X_ART_AvF)
          X_aovART_AvF_pval <- X_aovART_AvF["particle_source","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value
          allPM_ruralinterior_isa_art_pvals[i,2] <- X_aovART_AvF_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
        
        # pairwise comparisons
            art.con_X_ART <- art.con(X_ART_AvF,~particle_source, adjust="none")
            art.con_X_ART <- as.data.frame(art.con_X_ART)
            letters <- cldList(
            p.value ~ contrast,
            data = art.con_X_ART,
            threshold = 0.05  )
              
              # placing these results into our matrix
            
                # pairwise comparisons
                allPM_ruralinterior_isa_art_pairwise[i,2] <- art.con_X_ART$p.value[1]
                
                # letters
                allPM_ruralinterior_isa_art_pairwise[i,3] <- letters$Letter[1]
                allPM_ruralinterior_isa_art_pairwise[i,4] <- letters$Letter[2]  
                
    # Urban Edge
        
        # ART Anova
          formula <- as.formula(paste(all_element_names[i], "~ particle_source"))
          X_ART_AvF <- art(formula, data = urbanedge_isa[!is.na(urbanedge_isa[[element]]),])
          X_aovART_AvF <- anova(X_ART_AvF)
          X_aovART_AvF_pval <- X_aovART_AvF["particle_source","Pr(>F)"] # this extracts the p-value
          allPM_urbanedge_isa_art_pvals[i,2] <- X_aovART_AvF_pval # this places it into the matrix we made
        
        # pairwise comparisons
            art.con_X_ART <- art.con(X_ART_AvF,~particle_source, adjust="none")
            art.con_X_ART <- as.data.frame(art.con_X_ART)
            letters <- cldList(
            p.value ~ contrast,
            data = art.con_X_ART,
            threshold = 0.05  )
              
              # placing these results into our matrix
            
                # pairwise comparisons
```
